title: O God, Our Help in Ages Past
alternateTitles: Our God, Our Help in Ages Past
firstLine: O God, our help in ages past, our hope for years to come
tuneAttribution: Tune: ST. ANNE by William Croft, 1708.
publicDomainBasis: Isaac Watts (1674–1748), William Croft (1678–1727). Text published 1719 in Psalms of David. Both public domain.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.text:
O God, our help in ages past,
Our hope for years to come,
Our shelter from the stormy blast,
And our eternal home.

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.text:
Under the shadow of Thy throne
Still may we dwell secure;
Sufficient is Thine arm alone,
And our defense is sure.

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.text:
Before the hills in order stood,
Or earth received her frame,
From everlasting Thou art God,
To endless years the same.

sections.label: Verse 4
sections.text:
A thousand ages in Thy sight
Are like an evening gone;
Short as the watch that ends the night
Before the rising sun.

sections.label: Verse 5
sections.text:
Time, like an ever-rolling stream,
Bears all its sons away;
They fly, forgotten, as a dream
Dies at the opening day.

sections.label: Verse 6
sections.text:
O God, our help in ages past,
Our hope for years to come,
Be Thou our guard while troubles last,
And our eternal home.
